sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
All 4 buttons in row 33 of the right panel are now black (ON). The right panel row 33 shows: `WWWWWWBBBWWBBBWWBBBWWBBBWWWW`.
Now the right panel's row 33 matches the left panel's row 33 (all buttons ON).
But the level is not complete yet. Let me check if there's more to do.
Looking at the left panel:
- Row 33: all buttons black (ON)
- Row 39: all buttons light gray (OFF)
- Row 45: all buttons light gray (OFF)
And the right panel:
- Row 33: all buttons black (ON)
- Row 39: all buttons light gray (OFF)
- Row 45: all buttons light gray (OFF)
So the right panel's buttons now match the left panel's buttons. Maybe the level is complete now, or maybe I need to click the blue blob to complete it.
Let me try clicking the blue blob to see if the level is complete.
But wait, in Level 1, I clicked the blue blob after setting all buttons to OFF. In Level 2, maybe I need to set the buttons to a different configuration before clicking the blue blob.
Actually, let me also consider the left panel's object. In Level 2, the left panel has a dark gray object at the left side (cols 6-9, rows 16-19). Maybe I need to move this object to a different position before the level is complete.
Looking at the left blue square's pattern:
- Yellow cells at rows 57-59, cols 8-9
- White cells at row 58, cols 11 and 13
The yellow cells are at cols 8-9, which is the left side of the left blue square. The left panel's object is currently at the left side (cols 6-9). So the object's position matches the yellow cells' position.
But the white cells are at cols 11 and 13, which might indicate additional target positions. Maybe I need to move the object to those positions as well.
Actually, let me first try clicking the blue blob to see if the level is complete with the current configuration.

sections.content:

# Let me try clicking the blue blob to see if the level is complete
# The blue blob is at the bottom center, around rows 55-60, cols 28-35
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 57, 'col': 30}])
print(f"Result: {last_action_result}")
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")
print(f"Done: {last_action_result.get('done', False)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
